entry.title: Re: SAP CAP CDS Distinct does not return distinct values
entry.content:
Hello @catano,

In the SAPUsers there is the following data: 

[
{
 userId: "Test1"; 
userName: "Test User"; 
email: "test@user.de";
},
{
 userId: "Test1"; 
userName: "Tääst User"; 
email: "täst@user.de";
},
{
 userId: "Test1"; 
userName: "Tessst User"; 
email: "tessst@user.de";
},
{
 userId: "Test1"; 
userName: "Teßt User"; 
email: "teßt@user.de";
},
]

As you can see in my example, for the user ID Test1, there are 4 different entries in SAPUsers since the user was manually created and they made some typos or didnt know how to correctly write the name / email. 

In my DistinctUsers view I only want the first entry that is available for this user id to be shown. So I want to have distinct values by the ID (i.e. Test1) and the first username and email that is found in the database. Like the following:  

[
{
 userId: "Test1", 
userName: "Test User", 
email: "test@user.de"
}
]

entry.title: Re: Where is the field SBGRP(transaction FD33) in transac...
entry.content:
Hi,

SBGRP is not supported in FSCM. In transaction UKM_BP you can use Relationships. See KBA ##3028011 for further info:
KNKK-SBGRP (Credit representative group for credit management)
Should be maintained under relationships with type TUKMSBG. It would be saved in BUT050 table.
Also, for different selection purposes on Credit Profile you find Customer Credit Group option, for which you can define own groups as well.

Hope this helps.
Regards,
Emöke
SAP Support

entry.title: Try it out now : SAP Dynamic Forms generally available now in SAP BTP Trail Account
entry.content:
SAP Dynamic Forms - Trial edition is a trial subscription to the SAP Dynamic Forms BTP Application, an all-in-one solution that allows users to create, manage, and use digital forms that change in real-time as they are being filled out. Designers will be guided through the steps required to create a data-driven and responsive form, and users will be guided through the steps required to complete forms based on these designs.

Documentation Link : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DYNAMIC_FORMS?locale=en-US

SAP Dynamic forms Feature Scope : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DYNAMIC_FORMS/f9acfd39418a47cab622574ea9acaf84/7f9667d3844b46e6a3a9e17989dc675c.html?locale=en-US

SAP BTP Trial Account : https://cockpit.hanatrial.ondemand.com/trial/#

References :: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-dynamic-forms-1-0-released-features/ba-p/13748122

entry.title: Unable to approve PR/PO through email in S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
How to enable approve/reject button in email notification?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to approve PR/PO through email in S4 HANA publ...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer to the blog in SAP Community https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/how-to-set-up-the-email-notification-for-flexible-workflow-in-sap-s-4hana/bc-p/13524517

We can customise the email subject and the body. We also added the URL to navigate to the work item to My Inbox app. The receiver will click on the link that will open the related work item directly in the 'My Inbox' app 

However direct approver/rejection button in outlook functionality is not available.

SAP offers an opportunity for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers to submit improvement requests using this link: Homepage - Customer Influence (sap.com). Customer Influence (CIS) is a platform where customers can suggest features that they would like to see in future SAP HANA releases.

Please refer to the SAP KBA 0002963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product for more details.

Follow the below steps to open an Influence request:

  1. Get started by opening SAP Customer Influence.
  2. Register and accept the Terms of Use.
  3. Choose a session of your interest.
  4. Create and submit your improvement request.
  5. Take a look at existing improvement requests and start voting and commenting.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Script...
entry.content:
Hello Mohammed,

thank you for your detailed answer. However, I have the impression that with GUI scripting you are trying to simplify tasks that are not necessary at all when using PCA or others. Example: Your script for SCC4. For what reason? PCA and other tools export the SCC4 component (tables T000 and CLMS_TENANT) and then import it - with the effect that SCC4 then looks exactly as it did before. So what advantage does your script offer? Another example: Your script for RZ04. PCA and other tools export the OPMODE component (tables BTCOMSET, TPFBA, TPFID) and then perform the import, with the effect that RZ04 looks exactly the same as before - no need to run this transaction or do anything manually there.
The question for me is which procedure for the system refresh your article is based on: how are the users, the ALE configuration, the jobs, the SAP Office configuration, the RFC connections backed up and restored in your scenario, for example? Or are your scripts only intended for checking/viewing the system status after the system refresh?

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: How and where do you start a consulting career in SAP?
entry.content:
As a student, I am truly passionate about expanding my knowledge and developing new skills that will support my future career. I recently completed an end-user training course in SAP FI and CO, which not only gave me a solid foundation in these key modules but also fueled my enthusiasm for the SAP ecosystem. With this newfound understanding, I am eager to take the next step and pursue an official SAP certification.

Achieving this certification is a significant goal for me, as I believe it will open up numerous opportunities for professional growth and help me stand out in a competitive job market. I will work hard, continuously improving, and deepening my expertise in SAP. I hope to build a long-term career in this field, and I look forward to learning much more along this journey.

entry.title: How to send managed systems to the SLD and Solution Manager?
entry.content:
We face errors: The definition of Technical System 'NNN~HANADB' is not correct: 'NNN~HANADB': Installed Technical
System'NNN~HANADB~NNNNNN' must have at least one Server.

Cloud you please help to share the steps to register the HANA or other types of managed systems to the SLD or solman? Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to send managed systems to the SLD and Solution M...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

It's suggested to follow the guided answer: 3232202 - Guided Answer for Troubleshooting for missing or outdated LMDB data suppliers in SAP Solution Manager and SAP Focused Run.

For the HANA system, its full data is pushed by the HDBLCM tool on HANA server. In standard installations you can find the HDBLCM in the folder: /hana/shared/<SID>/hdblcm.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I’ve always believed that continuous learning is the key to success, especially in a fast-evolving field like technology. As a current student, I recently took the initiative to complete an SAP FI and CO end-user training course. This experience has sparked my curiosity and confirmed my desire to pursue SAP as a long-term career path. Now, I’m more determined than ever to take the next step: earning an official SAP certification.

This certification represents more than just a credential to me—it’s a gateway to countless opportunities and a chance to distinguish myself in the professional world. I am eager to immerse myself in the challenges of the certification process, knowing that the knowledge and skills I acquire will be invaluable in the future. SAP is a field with vast potential, and I’m excited to continue growing and learning as I navigate this rewarding journey.

entry.title: Re: Warehouse Transfer - issues to involve CO09 and BOP
entry.content:
Hello @Ghs1 

I think that what you are looking for is MRP planning between plants - SAP Help Stock Transfer from Plant to Plant

The process is very similar to regular MRP planning. The only difference is that the source of supply of a material is not purchasing or production but a transfer from another plant. The source plant is defined with special procurement key in MRP view of the material master data. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: BDBG - Error :- Data element not active
entry.content:
Hi All,

I did not get any help so thought of checking in debug how it is finding the structure - then came to know it is used method parameter type (structure to generate the Segment type and so on by prefixing Z1 to it).

We can solve the issue by creating the separate struture with the data element and method parameters must refer to this created structure instead of segment structure which will generate the ALE interface without any data element error.

entry.title: Navigating Mergers & Acquisitions, and Divestitures with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) can be transformative for companies, but they often bring complex challenges, especially for HR departments managing large-scale workforce integration. SAP SuccessFactors Services Architect Michael Müller hosted a webinar "Mergers & Acquisitions Powered by SAP SuccessFactors" on 10th October 2024 on how SAP SuccessFactors can streamline and simplify this process, turning potential headaches into opportunities for seamless growth.

Here are the key takeaways from the session:

  • Seamless User Provisioning & Authentication: When two companies come together, ensuring that new employees can securely access the company's systems is crucial. SAP SuccessFactors offers a unified platform for onboarding, which includes automated user provisioning and secure authentication. This ensures that new employees are quickly set up with access to the systems they need, allowing them to be productive from day one. Plus, the integration with existing identity management solutions ensures consistency in access control across the organization
  • Efficient Data Migration & Integration: One of the most complex aspects of M&A is transferring employee data from one company’s system to another. SuccessFactors simplifies this by providing powerful tools for migrating and integrating employee data. Whether the acquired company uses SAP or a non-SAP system, SuccessFactors offers templates for smooth data import, ensuring that the new employees' data is seamlessly incorporated into your existing HR system. The integration also covers the connection between payroll systems, time management, and other critical HR functions, helping to avoid disruptions during the transition

  •  Flexible Configurations for Success: Mergers often require adapting systems to new organizational structures, policies, and workflows. SuccessFactors offers tools to help HR teams easily modify system configurations to reflect the newly merged company’s needs. With its built-in Configuration Transport Center, HR teams can adjust settings and transfer them between systems with minimal effort. This flexibility ensures that the newly combined workforce is managed consistently and efficiently

  • Frameworks for M&A and Divestitures: While merging two companies is challenging, divesting parts of a company can be equally complex. SAP SuccessFactors not only supports M&A but also provides a robust toolset for divestitures. This includes copying data to new systems, purging unnecessary data from existing systems, and ensuring that the transition complies with data privacy regulations. SuccessFactors' tools ensure that whether you are acquiring or selling off parts of your business, the process is smooth and compliant, with minimal disruption to HR processes:

  • Real-world Success Stories: The webinar also highlighted examples of recent projects where companies successfully used SAP SuccessFactors to manage complex carve-out and acquisition scenarios. These case studies illustrated how SuccessFactors' data migration, user provisioning, and integration capabilities helped streamline M&A processes, even in industries with strict regulatory requirements, such as finance and healthcare.

Whether you're managing an acquisition, a merger, or a divestiture, SAP SuccessFactors provides the tools and frameworks to simplify the process, reduce risks, and ensure a smooth transition for both companies and employees. If you missed the live webinar, don’t worry! The recording is available for a limited time. Be sure to watch it and equip your HR team with the knowledge and tools to tackle your next M&A project confidently.

If you have specific needs to adjust your SAP SuccessFactors configuration for Mergers & Acquisitions, SAP HR experts can help with specific one-time, functional, or technical expertise, that is available on SAP Store to quickly get you the SAP Expertise you may be looking for.

 If you need holistic support on reviewing your SAP SuccessFactors and other HR application architecture for Mergers & Acquisitions, SAP HR experts can help with strategic and architectural advice. The service is to be ordered through your Customer Success Manager or Account Executive.

Additional Materials:

Webinar Recording

Slides shared during the webinar

Q&A Document

entry.title: #COE#BUMA CDS views are not elegible as data source in "Custom CDS Views" app.
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

"We require the CDS (I_MaintOrderTechObjCube) associated with the maintenance order, but it doesn't show up in the list of available data sources when creating a custom CDS. The required data source only appears when we change its recommended status from 'Yes' to 'No.' We attempted to use it as a data source but received a warning indicating that this CDS should not be utilized as a modeling data source. Can we proceed on using this as External API?

Regards!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Available-To-Promise
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the launch of our new e-learning course on SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Available-To-Promise. This course is designed to help you gain a comprehensive understanding of the features and functionalities within the SAP S/4HANA system that that provides real-time inventory visibility and allocation capabilities, allowing businesses to make more accurate and reliable delivery promises to customers.

This e-learning covers an introduction to SAP S/4HANA for advanced available-to-promise and helps you upskill on topics such as product availability check, backorder processing, business process scheduling, product allocation check and many more.

Goals at a glance:

  • Get an introduction to SAP S/4HANA for advanced available-to-promise.
  • Learn key topics around SAP S/4HANA for advanced available-to-promise such as product availability check, backorder processing, business process

Whether you are an existing SAP user or new to the platform, this course is designed to provide value to individuals at all skill levels.

Sign up for our SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Available-To-Promise e-learning course today and take the first step towards enhancing your skills and expertise in the SAP ecosystem.

Please note:
Before you can access the link to the e-learning, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

Access the e-learning here: SAP S/4HANA for Advanced Available-To-Promise

entry.title: Re: S/4Hana Cloud: Difference between "Central Purchasing...
entry.content:
Hi kaya-ya,

In SAP's Business Partner (BP) master data, particularly in the purchasing organization view, “Central Purchasing Block” and “Purchasing Block” serve different purposes and affect procurement processes in distinct ways.

Central Purchasing Block:

This block is applied globally to a Business Partner (BP) across all purchasing organizations.
When the Central Purchasing Block is set, the BP is blocked from procurement activities in any purchasing organization, essentially meaning no purchase orders or procurement-related transactions can be created for that BP across the entire system.
This type of block is managed centrally, and it impacts the vendor at a more comprehensive level.

Purchasing Block:
This block is applied at the level of a specific purchasing organization.
When the Purchasing Block is set, the Business Partner is restricted from procurement activities only within the purchasing organization where the block is set. Other purchasing organizations within the company can still conduct procurement transactions with the same BP if they do not have this block in place.
This block provides more granular control, allowing a BP to be active in some purchasing organizations but restricted in others.

BR Serhii.

For me, this certification is more than just a formal qualification; it’s a stepping stone towards mastering a field that I believe holds incredible potential for growth and innovation. I’m drawn to the versatility of SAP and its applications across industries, and I’m eager to immerse myself deeper into it. Passing the certification is a critical milestone that I’m determined to reach, as it will equip me with the tools I need to seize exciting opportunities and contribute meaningfully in the workplace.

I’m ready to put in the effort, face the challenges, and grow alongside the dynamic world of SAP. This is just the beginning of what I envision as a long and rewarding journey in this field.

Hopefully, someone of you knows what should be the input there.

Thank you!

entry.title: JIT schedules and FRC schedules occur both in stock requirement list for same period of time
entry.content:

Is this related to the schedule line category  ? and how to solve?

entry.title: Entry Frequency for a CX Journey
entry.content:
Hi team,

if I create an entry point based on profile attribute or activity indicator, how frequent is my CX journey going to be triggered? Is the entry point going to start daily? weekly? instantly? or based on the profile update? 

I added a condition based on an activity, and I am wondering when the CX journey will be triggered. is it:

  •  
  • Whenever the specific activity indicator or profile attribute are updated?
  •  
  • Does the journey check the indicators daily?weekly?..etc.
  •  
  • You can enhance the question for segmentation and profile attributes too.
  •  

 

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Entry Frequency for a CX Journey
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

Entry conditions define the entry point for each milestone and trigger the customer’s transition from one milestone to the next. They are set by defining a trigger that can be created with the following entities along with attributes (numerical, textual, or Boolean):

  • Segments (for example customers that bought an item of over $30 in the past 24 hours)
  • Activity indicators (for example a customer logged in)
  • Profile attributes (for example all customers over 65 years old living in Seattle, WA)

The customer’s experiences within a certain milestone are managed through CX flows that can be triggered by a single event, or by a series of events.

 

Please refer the following documentations below:

CX Journeys

Triggers

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Re: Can we use fiori app for master data upload

entry.title: Re: System copy PRD to TST - Charm pre-requites required
entry.content:
Hello,

how are you doing the system refresh (or more precise, the pre- and post-processing)? When using PCA (or another tool), usually a component CHARM_CTS (in my test system this is a collection of 21 tables) will be exported in front of the system refresh and imported afterwards. With that, there should be nothing to be done anymore.

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Goal Plan with sub-sub milestones
entry.content:
Hi masters, It´s possible to create a goal plan similar to this?

The issue is, that from each goal (colum A), there are a serie of sugoals (milestones, colum C), and each of these subgoals has sub-subgoals. It´s possible to obtain something similar to these?

Although another idea occurred to me. Take each objective to goal plan level (column A would be goal plan OG1). Then criteria of measure (column C), would be the name of the objective, with a weight (column B), and each column D, would be a milestone, with its field of observations, and the value fields. Compliance (column that says 79.31%) would be on each milestone, and thus I could surely get the total correctly for each goal (criteria of measure).

How do you see it? Any other ideas? What do you think, master @SachinPachauri

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM RF with Screen Personas - Viewpoint functiona...
entry.content:
Hi tamas_hoznek, , sorry I was on vacation, so my feedback is a little late.

I have done the cleanup (compressing and deleting undo history). This has no effect. I have also tried a new flavor. I have the same problem there too. The problem only occurred after I implemented the note 3517231. On another S/4 system, on which the note is not implemented, I do not have this behavior.

Best Regards Simon

entry.title: How to get assigned User Group Information of BTP login user in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to create Approval form using SAP Build Apps.

And the functions added in the app can be used based on user roles.

I did not see any place to add user role in Cloud Identity Services app.

The first question I would like to ask is that

1. Is anywhere to create user role in Cloud Identity Services app?

Currently, I planned to create 2 groups for user roles and will assign to each user.

 

The second question I would like to ask is that

2. Is it possible to retrieve the assigned group of login user in SAP Build Apps?

If yes, how can I retrieve? 

I did not find any useful documentation until now.

So, If you have any information about it, could you please share with me?

 

And all the above workarounds are not possible to do so,

3. Is there any workaround to assign User Role based on BTP login authentication?

Thank You.

Best regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: How to find all my blog posts in SAP Community 2024
entry.content:
Log in SAP Community.

1. and 2. Click on your "profile stack" then on "My content"

3. Click on your "big letter"

4. Here are your blog posts:

entry.title: Re: B1iF Message Failure: Outbound FIE001 basePath or pattern is 'null' java.io.IOException
entry.content:
Hello SGE,

To test the issue, please try starting the SAP Integration Service using a different user account. The Local System account has only local permissions and cannot access network paths. By running the service under an account with the necessary network permissions, you may resolve the access problem.

This should help and solve the issue.

entry.title: Odata service that returns data to the online store
entry.content:
Hello,
I would like to create an Odata service that retrieves data for a customer in SAP.
I'm fairly new to this topic and don't know my way around yet.
The data should then be displayed in our online store.

The customer number and email of the customer are also transferred from the online store in the URL so that the corresponding customer can be found.

Do you have any ideas on how exactly I can implement this?

I would also like to know how the parameters from the URL can be used in the SQL query.

Thank you very much!

Stefan

entry.title: Repetition of the Multiday class multiple times in the output report
entry.content:
Hi Members,

I have created one class for 3 different days. However, in the output report the class is coming multiple times. 

What could be the reason behind such error?

entry.title: Re: Can we use fiori app for master data upload
entry.content: Earlier we used BDC for these TCODES-COB1, and VBN1

entry.title: How to make my email address public?
entry.content:
I want to share my email with my SAP Community "Friends" (I have chosen my friends via the Private Messages (button "Messages") then select a message from someone and click on Add Friend). How to make it visible to them, where can they see my email address?

Currently, I have set this below option (top big letter "S" > Settings > Preferences > Privacy > Show email address to > Friends only), but my friends still can't see my email address anywhere:

Is it also possible to do the same for the whole community if needed? Will this option work: "Show email address to > All"?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David @David_Chaviano 

My name is Nguyet. I am a minority woman from Vietnam. In September, I asked for your support to get a free exam voucher. I received it and passed the SAP Private Cloud certification in production management. I truly appreciate your dedicated help. As I shared with you before, I have only recently changed industries and have a strong interest in SAP. Passing the recent certification has further reinforced my belief in the path I’ve chosen. Although I understand it might be difficult for you to consider, I sincerely hope you can provide me with a second exam voucher. I plan to further expand my SAP learning and hope to make new strides in my career. I kindly ask you to think about this request. Once again, thank you so much for your support.

entry.title: Re: How to update custom field info at item level of cont...
entry.content:
Check the documentation of the BAPI there is a list of the structures managed  in EXTENSIONIN 

  • BAPI_TE_WCOCOH[X]
  • BAPI_TE_WCOCOI[X]
  • BAPI_TE_KOMG[X]
  • BAPI_TE_BVB
  • BAPI_TE_CAL
  • BAPI_TE_KONP[X]

(AFAIK BAPI_TE_VT_I is obsolete)

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve data from a fieldymbol nested of deep...
entry.content: Thank you again Juan. With your hint I could solve the problem:

entry.title: Re: ERROR: Upgrading @cap/cds and @cap/cds-dk to version 8
entry.content:
Hey @BastiMankel ,

first of all, when working with @sap/cds@8.3.1 and @sap/cds-dk@8.3.0 everything works except for the $metadata request.

After debugging through your sample code with @hose versions I saw, that the metadata error is cause by the metadata middleware (@sap/cds/libx/odata/middleware/metadata.js => line 66). This relies on having either cds.context.model or cds.model being properly filled, which is not the case.

I couldn't find out, why both of which are undefined, especially since the `service` variable shows, that the model is properly loaded.

Tweeking line 66 of the metadata.js from 

const csnService = (cds.context.model || cds.model).definitions[service.definition.name]

to

const csnService = (cds.context.model || cds.model || service.model).definitions[service.definition.name]

makes the metadata work again, but that is something, that needs to be addressed to the SAP team working on @sap/cds or SAP Cloud SDK, I think. 

When I tried comparing the differences in that metadata middleware between @sap/cds@7.9.3 and @sap/cds@8.3.1, I noticed, that middleware is not activated in version 7.9.3 but is in 8.3.1 without having done anything within your project setup. Unfortunately, I did not find any hint on that behaviour change in the CAPire docs.

Looking forward to seeing any progress on that topic since at the moment it's blocking our migration to CAPv8...

entry.title: Promotion in Analyze Promotions App is not editable
entry.content:
Hello,

I have an issue that promotion, created in Analyze Promotions app is not editable, pencil is greyed out.

I faced earlier such issue for one of the users, but it was question of permissions. Now it's greyed out for me as well, although I have authorizations of administrator.

Do you know what could be the root cause?

Mariia

entry.title: Re: Unable to create a technical user in SAP HANA cockpit
entry.content: Possible reasons for this issue include user permissions, password policy, user limit, system resources, and errors. Ensure your user has necessary privileges, your password meets SAP HANA's policy, you haven't reached the user limit, your system has enough resources, and check for error messages in system logs. If issues persist, contact SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Nice keyboard! Did you build it yourself?

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve data from a fieldymbol nested of deep...
entry.content:
The solution looks a bit different, but anyhow your "LOOPS" gave me the idea:

 

 

data:  ls_value_tab   type ty_table_line,
          lt_value_tab   type table of ty_table_line,
          ls_response    type ty_basetype,
          lt_response    type standard table of ty_basetype,
          lv_count       type i.

    field-symbols: <data>        type any,
                   <data2>       type any table,
                   <objects>     type any,
                   <object>      type any,
                   <properties>  type any,
                   <property>    type any,
                   <id>          type string,
                   <type>        type string,
                   <cardinality> type string,
                   <value_tab>   type any table.

assign lo_json_data->* to <data>.
        assign component 'OBJECTS' of structure <data> to <data2>.

        if sy-subrc = 0 and <data2> is assigned.

          loop at <data2> assigning <objects>.
            assign component 'OBJECT' of structure <objects> to <object>.

            if sy-subrc = 0 and <object> is assigned.
              assign component 'PROPERTIES' of structure <object> to <properties>.

              if sy-subrc = 0 and <properties> is assigned.
                do.
                  lv_count = lv_count + 1.
                  assign component lv_count of structure <properties> to <property>.
                  if sy-subrc <> 0 or  not <property> is assigned.
                    exit.
                  else.
                    assign component 'ID'          of structure <property> to <id>.
                    assign component 'TYPE'        of structure <property> to <type>.
                    assign component 'CARDINALITY' of structure <property> to <cardinality>.

                    if <cardinality> = 'single'.
                      assign component 'VALUE' of structure <property> to field-symbol(<value>).
                    else.
                      assign component 'VALUE' of structure <property> to <value_tab>.
                    endif.

                    ls_response-id          = <id>.
                    ls_response-type        = <type>.
                    ls_response-cardinality = <cardinality>.
                    if ls_response-cardinality = 'single'.
                      ls_value_tab-tline = <value>.
                      append ls_value_tab to lt_value_tab.
                      ls_response-value = lt_value_tab.
                    else.
                      loop at <value_tab> assigning <value>.
                        ls_value_tab-tline = <value>.
                         append ls_value_tab to lt_value_tab.
                      endloop.
                      ls_response-value = lt_value_tab.
                    endif.
                    clear: lt_value_tab[], ls_value_tab.
                    append ls_response to lt_response.
                  endif.
                enddo.
              endif.
            endif.
          endloop.
​

entry.title: Re: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
entry.content:
'Monitor' & 'Execute' permission for shared environment

@florian_buech  Seems only  'Execute' permission is needed, is it true?

entry.title: Re: Variant configuration non material parameters more
entry.content:
Normally, there are two critical parts for VC.

1. Which according the Variant continuation choose.

That will be refer to the  constraints  setting, you should set the dependency for the different characteristic.

For example.

CT04 create the characteristic.

PC_Model (X1 X3)

PC_HARDDISK (250G 500G)

PC_MEMORY (4G 8G 16G)

Then create a class (TYPE=300) to combine the characteristics. （T-code:CL02）

And assign the Class to the material, and when you choose the PC_MODEL, the system can decide the Value of PC_MEMORY if you set the constrains.

2. Create a supper BOM, which contains all the possible materials list.

EX.

ITEM 0010： Material = 250GDISK 

Then set the Pre-condition in  OD for material 250GDISK ( PC_HARDDISK = '250G' ), so when you choose the PC_HARDDISK = '250G' in Sales order, this component item will be chosen.

ITEM 0020： Material = 500GDISK

Then set the Pre-condition in  OD for Material 500GDISK ( PC_HARDDISK = '500G' ), so when you choose the PC_HARDDISK = '500G' in Sales order, this component item will be chosen.

For the VC, there are so many information, that you can check the SAP Help to learn more.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/a73402f511734e6eac56063e631bf24e/1a40b953495bb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: SAP Presents AI Innovations for the Energy Sector at Enlit Europe in Milan
entry.content:
SAP will be attending Enlit Europe in Milan this year. The event brings together industry leaders, experts and innovators to discuss the latest trends and technologies in the energy sector.

Our utilities experts Mateu Munar and Thomas Mueller will be joining Enlit Europe and will be available for customer and partner discussions. They will be on hand to talk about SAP's latest solutions and innovations in the energy sector and how they can help companies achieve greater efficiency and innovation. Please reach out to them via the Enlit app. 

SAP will not only be available for talks but will also participate in the “AI Turbocharger Session”: AI boosting efficiency and accelerating innovation”, taking place in the Digitalisation Hub in Hall 5 on October 23 at 10:30am. During this session, SAP will have the opportunity to demonstrate how its AI solutions are helping to revolutionize the energy industry.

 

If you are attending Enlit Europe in Milan, be sure to get in touch with my colleagues and arrange a meeting with Mateu Munar and Thomas Mueller. With their expertise and SAP's innovative solutions, you will be well equipped.

Find more general information on the Enlit Europe website.

Stay tuned for future updates and please continue sharing your thoughts with us!

Service broker error: Service broker pr-service-broker-devfs-2395c89e-82cf-4af0-bc4a-52357f928245 failed with: Error while provisioning service: [object Object]

entry.title: Self-confident on stage: How I learned to manage my Inner Critic
entry.content:
Here I was, standing in front of my customer’s project team, of whom I didn’t know anyone besides the project manager. My task: inspire those 30 people for the upcoming software project. I have held presentations like this countless times. I could almost pray them down in sleep - and yet: every time: this nasty fluffy feeling in my stomach.

I often wondered where this feeling of insecurity came from. In my research, I came across many interesting theories, including the theme of the “Inner Critic”, “Inner Saboteur” or “Inner Script”. This theory describes, so to speak, an “inner voice” that makes us show a certain behavior or prevents us from doing certain things. Often the inner critic arises from past experiences, fears, or deeply rooted uncertainties. And of course it is not only linked to public speaking, but holistically to our lives and social interactions.

Inner critics can arise, for example, from beliefs. These are assumptions deeply anchored in our subconscious mind, which may come, for example, from our childhood or fundamentally from experiences with other people. Beliefs can certainly be helpful if, for example, they prescribe us to behave in a socially compliant way. For example, if you often hear “Be quiet! Hold back!” from your parents or teachers in your childhood, you might internalize: “I must not be loud.” This can be helpful in dosed use – for example in a learning environment at school. However, if you internalize this belief too strongly, it can mean that even in adulthood you generally (and unconsciously) withhold yourself or reluctantly express your opinion against others.

What makes things more difficult is that you do not only live with just one, but with a whole series of beliefs and internal critics – so the challenge can be multifaceted.

Luckily, the power of beliefs and the resulting internal critics can also be defused. In a first step, it is important to become aware of your beliefs and how your inner critic affects you.  Once identified, you can start to question what your saboteur asks of you and possibly set an end to self-fulfilling prophecies and vicious circles that hinder your performance. As a result, you transform negative beliefs into realistic self-images and thus act more freely and confidently – not only on stage.

Calming and managing your Inner Critic can be learned. One workshop has helped me a lot, which we now offer at SAP Women in Tech as a part of our SAP Women in Tech Speaker Journey. In this workshop, we create awareness of the related psychological mechanisms. The participants learn how to get on to their personal inner saboteur and acquire strategies to hold it at bay.  Of course, it takes willpower and repeated training to free oneself from practiced behavioral patterns, but it is indeed worth the hard work.

For my part, I learned over time to recognize when my personal saboteur sneaked up. With some practice, I can now deliberately silence him. For me personally, I figured that presenting more often and actively looking for opportunities to do so helped tremendously. Once the fluffy feeling in my stomach comes up, the positive feedback and affirmations of the audience encourage me, calm my inner critic, and make my presentations ever more confident.

What beliefs influence you when presenting? How do you deal with your inner saboteur? Do you have strategies on how to silence him (or at least make him “whisper”)? I am looking forward to your tips and inspirations!

entry.title: Re: 🟢 What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Roger, 

you can expect that more and more Fiori apps are supporting the adaptation mode. Generally speaking all apps built with Fiori Elements do support the adaptation mode. For freestyle SAPUI5 apps, it depends on the controls. If they use so-called "smart controls" these controls also support the adaptation mode. So in these apps you will find sections that support the UI adaptation mode while other areas do not.

Thomas

.

entry.title: Public Cloud ))) No default value maintained for op. generation for order type ybm1
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Manufacturing 

 

Hello, I'm leaving a question because there is an error that I can't solve while practicing SAP PUBLIC CLOUD Manufacturing.

Error during conversion from planned order to production order.

When I searched for the co831 error, they told me to go into OPJG or SPRO. But as you know, there is no T-CODE like that in PUBLIC CLOUD. On a lighter note, I know only ca010203, cs010203, cr010203, co010203 

 

For your information, the picture below is DEFECT VALUE and only DISPLAY MODE was possible. 

Please let me know how I can apply ORDER TYPE YBM1 to my material (FERT_BDS_단팥빵).

Thank you for reading.

entry.title: Re: Issue with sap.ui.core.Component.create and sap.ui.fl...

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Modernization: Percentage Breakdown of Project Phases
entry.content:
Introduction

Introduction

In a recent session on SAP Integration Suite modernization, we received a detailed percentage breakdown of project phases, including Technical Interface Migration, Testing, Partner Communication, and Cutover. While these estimates offer a helpful starting point, the specifics can vary greatly depending on various factors. This blog will explore the key aspects that can dramatically shift these percentages and affect the overall project outcome.

The Devil Lies in the Details

During one of the recent sessions on SAP Integration Suite modernization hosted by CBS (special thanks to Stefan Münchow), the team presented a breakdown of project phases, including Technical Interface Migration, Testing, Partner Communication, and Cutover (refer to Figure 1).

Figure 1 - Percentage Breakdown of Project Phases of SAP Integration Suite migration by CBS (Stefan Munchow)

However, this breakdown significantly differs from insights provided by Gartner in the article "How to Successfully Migrate to a New Integration Platform" by Abhishek Singh (October 1, 2021 but no longer available), which noted, "Testing is a substantial part of the migration effort, consuming around 60% to 65% of the overall work." How do we reconcile the discrepancy between estimates ranging from 25% to 60-65%? Which numbers are more accurate?

Figure 2 – 65% or 25% or maybe both?

It Depends...

My family often jokes about my frequent response, "It depends," and in this case, it rings true. Several factors can explain the large variance in these numbers, and I will break them down in detail.

Scope of the SAP Integration Suite Program

One key factor that can affect the percentage breakdown is whether we are accounting for the development team's efforts alone or including everyone involved in the project. For example, if a Service Integrator or Global Service Integrator presents a project breakdown, they may focus only on their responsibilities, excluding tasks like Functional Testing, User Acceptance Testing, or Partner Communication. These tasks typically fall under the company’s project management team.

Testing performed by developers who know the interfaces may only take 25% of the development effort, but if you account for Functional Testing and User Acceptance Testing—where users must test all scenarios—testing could take up 60-65% of the overall work. Similarly, Partner Communication efforts, especially when involving hundreds or thousands of EDI partners, could stretch out over months, not days.

Figure 3 - Ask questions on the scope  

Important Tip:
When reviewing a percentage breakdown of project phases, ask whether internal resources (functional consultants) and coordination with external B2B partners for communication tests are included.

Type of SAP Integration Suite Migration (Lift and Shift vs. Modernization)

With lift and shift (and all the automation related to that: Elevate from SAP Process Orchestration and Modernize your integration platform with SAP Integration Suite and Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration) it’s hard to imagine the development effort would be 50%  (unless migrations would take a few days) but when you do a mix (lift and shift with a mix of new functions like use of AEM scenarios) then 50% of the development scope (not entire modernization as per the previously mentioned chapter) all of a sudden sounds reasonable. Some of my thoughts on this topic can also be found here: SAP BTP Integration Suite migration: don't do lift&shift!

Size of the SAP Integration Landscape (Number of Interfaces)

The size of your SAP integration landscape (e.g., 50-200 vs. 500-5000 interfaces) can greatly affect the percentage breakdown of project phases.

Figure 4 - Size is important 

Why should the percentage Breakdown of Project Phases of SAP Integration Suite matter because of the size?

The bigger you are the more important the project – that means that large (500-5000 interfaces migration projects) tend to be larger investments which means the company might be (does not mean it’s always the case) willing to invest more to make sure all goes well = potentially more skilled resources, more experience project management, more authority to push functional and business teams for testing. Is that always the case? Not at all but it might influence the percentage breakdown.

The more important your company the easier it is to communicate with partners – imagine doing an SAP Integration Suite migration being a one country company connected with 3-5 biggest retailers. Do you think they will easily allocate anytime easily? On the other hand imagine you’re a big retail company or one of the largest FMCG companies – do you think they will struggle as much with communication with their partners when they need to change something?

Important Tip:
When discussing the breakdown of project phases with a Service Integrator, be clear about your company's influence when negotiating with B2B partners.

Test Automation vs. Manual Testing

Testing manually for smaller projects (up to 50-100 interfaces) is feasible but inefficient. Automating the testing process can save significant time. Solutions like SAP's recommended automation tools - Automate over 65% of your SAP Integration Suite migration with Int4 solutions  and SAP services - Accelerate move to SAP Integration Suite with SAP Integration Suite Testing Service, can reduce testing time by over 65%, making a compelling case for automation in SAP Integration Suite migrations. They also support the latest automated migraton concepts - SAP Pipeline Concept and B2B TPM testing. 

Important Tip:
Consider automating the testing of SAP Integration Suite migrations. Learn more about testing automation through resources like the Int4 Shield Crash Course here.

Summary

The percentage breakdown of project phases in SAP Integration Suite modernisation projects can vary dramatically based on factors like the scope of the program, the type of migration, the size of the integration landscape, and whether testing is automated or manual. These factors can significantly alter the time and resources needed for phases such as Testing and Partner Communication, resulting in percentages ranging from 25% to 65%. Properly understanding and addressing these elements ensures a more accurate estimation of project efforts, reducing surprises and enabling smoother project execution.

entry.title: Learn how to get an S-user ID
entry.content:
SAP S-user ID, or S-user, is a unique identifier that allows you to login to your SAP products, report a case, download software, configure connections, and more. Your S-user ID contains the letter ‘S’ followed by a string of numbers (e.g. S00*******). 

You may come across the term ‘Super administrator’ and wonder how that differs from S-user. The super administrator within the SAP product your company is using (e.g. SAP Analytics Cloud) encompasses all administrative duties and has t highest level of authorization. The Super administrator is usually the person in your organization who originally purchased the SAP product. 

 

How are S-user IDs assigned? 

For new customers, the individual who purchased the SAP product will receive a welcome email containing their S-user ID. In this case, SAP assigns the first S-user ID. 

 

 The Super administrator can assign S-user IDs to various people in their organization, such as managers, who may need admin-level status. Everyone else in the company who uses the product does not necessarily need to have S-user IDs, they can simply login using their work email address once the Super administrator or S-user grants them access. 

 

 Please note, SAP cannot assign additional S-user IDs on your company’s behalf. 
 
If your organization has only one Super Administrator who has left, please contact the Customer Interaction Center to nominate a new Super Administrator. Ensure to provide the following information: 
 

 Ensure that the new nominee has below details: 

S-user ID (if already created): 

First name: 

Surname: 

Email address (note- domain of the email id should match company name): 

Phone number with country code: 

Designation: 

Reason for new super administrator nomination. 

 
To ensure that a new Super Admin can be nominated and granted Super Admin rights, please confirm that your organization has a valid maintenance agreement with SAP and that the installation is active. 

 

Let’s look at the steps, as how the company’s super admin creates an S-user ID. 

  1. Logon to SAP for Me and go to User Management  
  2. Click Request User on the top of the application 

     3. Fill in the Request User form 

  1. Click Submit 
  2. User was requested, click OK 
  • The user request is not instant and may take some time to complete. 
  • The new user will receive an e-mail from SAP Notification Services with the new S-user ID and an activation link, which is valid for 10 days. 
  • You can check the status of the new user on the Requested Users tab. Once the user is complete it will move to the Users tab. 
  • After the user is created, click the user ID and add a phone number or change any available details if needed. 
  • Assign authorizations to the user ID (see KBA 1511008). 

 

 

How to delete S-user IDs 

  1. In the User Management application, go to the Users tab 
  2. Scroll down the list or use the filter feature to find the S-user ID(s) to be deleted 
  3. Click the checkbox in front of the user(s) 
  4. Click Delete (if an error appears around important functions see KBA 1271517) 

 

 

  • It may take up to 24 hours to process your request for deletion. 
  • Deleted S-user IDs are listed in the Deleted Users tab of the application for twelve months and cannot be reactivated (KBA 1811859). 
  • Only a user that holds Super Administrator rights can delete another Super Administrator. 
  • S-user ID with important function (or critical roles) can be deleted only once those functions have been transferred to another S-user ID within the same company ERP number (KBA 1271517). 
  • Every customer account requires at least one Super Administrator. Therefore, you cannot delete all Super Administrators under your account. In exceptional cases when there is no active maintenance linked to your account, all Super Administrators can be deleted by SAP, provided you Account Executive confirms the request. 

For issues or questions on this topic, report a case for component XX-SER-FORME. Be sure to include details, S-user ID, screenshots, etc. to assist in troubleshooting. 

  • Online help for User, Authorization, and Administrator Concept 
  • KBA 2443869 - S-user ID authorization guide - SAP for Me 
  • KBA 1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me 
  • KBA 1282854 - Information on adding/editing/deleting authorizations for S-user IDs - SAP for Me 
  • KBA 1614304 - How to use the Copy User's Authorization feature for S-user IDs - SAP for Me 

 

 

And now let’s check on how you can find the Super Admin details of your company 

  • Just go to me.sap.com and then >’Users &Contacts’ > My Important Contacts and you will see the list of all the S-users with special roles for each installation. 

 

If you want to make changes in the list of Important Contacts, please see these KBAs: 
 
How to maintain Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me 

How to change Software Recipient and Technical Contact users for on-premise installations listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me 

Thanks for reading!   
  
We would love to hear from you, let us know what you think? 😊 

 If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community   

 

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: How to create new fields for journal entries mass upload in public cloud?
entry.content: May I check if Long Text field is still available in upload general JE tile?

entry.title: Localized entity data not showing translated texts
entry.content:
I am developing a CAP application which has simple Employee - Department relationship.
 
Employee entity
entity Employees {
    key id        : String(15);
        firstname : String(25);
        lastname  : String(25);
        gender    : Association to Gender;
        dob: Date;
        email: String;
        phone: Phone;
        department: Association to Department;
}
 
Department entity
@Fiori.draft.enabled
entity Department: cuid {
    key name: String;
        description: localized String
}
 
The department entity can hold description in different languages.
Managing department entity
But when I try to display the Employee data along with the translated department description, it is not showing anything.
Annotation.cds
 
annotate service.Employees with @(
    UI.FieldGroup #GeneratedGroup : {
        $Type : 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [
// Some redacted code for Object Page
 
        ],
    },
    UI.Facets : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            ID : 'GeneratedFacet1',
            Label : 'General Information',
            Target : '@UI.FieldGroup#GeneratedGroup',
        },
    ],
    UI.LineItem : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Label : 'Partner Id',
            Value : id,
            ![@UI.Importance] : #High,
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Label : 'First Name',
            Value : firstname,
            ![@UI.Importance] : #High,
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Label : 'Last Name',
            Value : lastname,
            ![@UI.Importance] : #High,
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Label : 'Gender',
            Value : gender_gender,
            ![@UI.Importance] : #High,
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : department.description,
            Label : 'Department',
        },
    ],
);
 
annotate service.Employees with {
    department @(
        Common.ValueList : {
        $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
        CollectionPath : 'Department',
        Parameters : [
            {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                LocalDataProperty : department_name,
                ValueListProperty : 'name',
            },
            {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
                ValueListProperty : 'description',
            }
        ],
        Label : 'Department',
    },
        Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : true,
    )
};
 
annotate service.Employees with {
    organization @Common.ValueList : {
        $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
        CollectionPath : 'Organization',
        Parameters : [
            {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                LocalDataProperty : organization_ID,
                ValueListProperty : 'ID',
            },
            {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
                ValueListProperty : 'name',
            },
        ],
    }
};
 
annotate service.Employees with {
    employment @(
        Common.ValueList : {
        $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
        CollectionPath : 'EmploymentType',
        Parameters : [
            {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
                LocalDataProperty : employment_name,
                ValueListProperty : 'name',
            },
            {
                $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
                ValueListProperty : 'description',
            },
        ],
        Label : 'Employment',
    },
        Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : true,
    )
};
 
annotate service.Department with {
    name @Common.Text : description
};
 
 
 
Service.cds
 
 
service AdminService {
    @odata.draft.enabled
    entity Employees as projection on schema.Employees;
    @odata.draft.enabled
    entity Department as projection on schema.Department;
}

entry.title: How can I check Project Profiles in SAP Public CLoud?
entry.content: I want to check Project Profiles Config in Public Cloud, from Previous blogs I have read on this topic, tells me that, Project Profile cannot be configured but I think, I should still be able to display the existing Profiles.

entry.title: How to create BRF+ for SAP21 FI Customer Invoice ?
entry.content:
Hi All,

i am getting the above error, How can I solve this.

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: shopfloor routing app - Execute migration report for...
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu 

As per the message long text migration tasks are not fully processed and that prevents access to routing apps:

Check the status of the migration tasks with the MPE_RTG_DATA_MIGRATION report.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Apps usage report for SAP Fiori launchpad (Monitoring SAP Fiori applications)
entry.content:
Hi,
Do you know if we can track the fiori apps built with Odata V4 services?

Regards,

Ramya

entry.title: Re: Idea for improvement: Filter for this year's past Devtoberfest events in List view
entry.content:
Hi @marcel_schork and  @Amaury_Van-Espen

Please change view and use "calendar option".  This is a better option for navigating old events.

entry.title: Re: Finding Tax Rate for Custom CDS View
entry.content: it doesn't seem to work for me, but thanks for your help

entry.title: Re: Transitioning to SapMachine: Preparing for the Future of SAP JVM in SAP Focused Run
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" As we move forward, we highly encourage all SAP customers to begin planning their migration to SapMachine as soon as possible. While the shift from SAPJVM 8 to SapMachine represents a significant change, it also offers a great opportunity to modernize and secure your Java environments for the long term. "

We're all ready to go. ( & good to go )

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori.
